summary: KD Trust dashboard project with AI Hub (React). Internet Identity integration planned in 3 steps: Step 1 (frontend-only auth), Step 2 (backend Motoko favorites), Step 3 (wire frontend to backend). Deployment issues encountered; attempting stepwise implementation.
architectural_notes: Uses ic-use-internet-identity hook for Internet Identity authentication
Frontend built with React; AI Hub already converted to React component
Deployed on ICP (Internet Computer); Motoko backend canisters
known_issues: Builder times out on complex/large builds; stepwise incremental approach required

existing_features.title: Add a login gate to the KD Trust page (KDTrustPage.tsx): when the user is not authenticated via Internet Identity, display a prominent centered prompt with a 'Login with Internet Identity' button using the existing useInternetIdentity hook.

Update the Layout.tsx navigation header to display the authenticated user's principal (truncated, e.g. first 8 and last 4 characters) and a Logout button when the user is logged in via Internet Identity, using the existing useInternetIdentity hook. The logout button should call the logout function from that hook.

Allow the KD Trust page to display the AI Hub and dashboard content in guest mode (unauthenticated), with favorites falling back to localStorage via the existing useKDTrustFavorites hook. Show a non-blocking banner or card prompting login for personalized favorites rather than blocking all content.

new_feature_requests.summary: Internet Identity frontend auth (login gate, principal display, logout button)
Backend Motoko per-user favorites storage (HashMap<Principal, UserData> with addFavorite/removeFavorite/getFavorites)
Connect frontend favorites to backend after II login
